rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL ERIN TOMREN VS. FINANCIAL INDUSTRY REGULATORY AUTHORITY
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 2, 2019, line 1. PETITIONER MICHAEL TOMREN'S Notice Of Hearing. Petitioner Michael Tomren's petition to correct arbitration award is denied. Mr. Tomren has failed to show that any of the limited statutory grounds for correction of an arbitration award under Code of Civil Procedure § 1286.6 is applicable. "Generally, courts cannot review arbitration awards for errors of fact or law, even when those errors appear on the face of the award or cause substantial injustice to the parties." (Richey v. AutoNation, Inc. (2015) 60 Cal.4th 909, 916.) "More specifically, courts will not review the validity of the arbitrator's reasoning." (Moncharsh v. Heily & Blase (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1, 11.) The relief sought by Mr. Tomren affects the merits of the arbitrator's award and does not meet the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure § 1286.6(b). Mr. Tomren has failed to show that the arbitrator exceeded his powers and is not entitled to any relief pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 1286.2(a)(4). The arbitrator was not required to give reasons for denying the request for expungement of the records for occurrences 148690 and 1520997. Nor will the Court speculate as to the reasoning and grounds for the arbitrator's decision to deny these requests based on a single remark made by the arbitrator. (See Barnes v. Logan (9th Cir. 1997) 122 F.3d 820, 825 ["[i]t is not for this court to speculate, based on the sparse record and slim arguments, as to the [arbitration] panel's reasoning."].) That is particularly so here, where Petitioner has failed to provide the Court with a transcript or recording of the evidentiary hearing. Furthermore, even an erroneous decision as to the grounds for expungement of the records would not show that the arbitrator exceeded his jurisdiction. (See Safari Associates v. Superior Court (2014) 231 Cal.App.4th 1400, 1403-04 ["California law is clear that 'arbitrators do not "exceed[ ] their powers" . . . merely by rendering an erroneous decision on a legal or factual issue, so long as the issue was within the scope of the controversy submitted to the arbitrators.'"]). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for FINRA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JEAN-YVES LENDORMY VS. KYLE HANSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Hearing On Plaintiffs Renewed Motion To Correct Amend Judgment [Ccp 473(D)]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 2, 2019, line 3. PLAINTIFF JEAN-YVES LENDORMY'S Renewed Motion To Correct Amend Judgment [Ccp 473(D)]. Plaintiff Jean-Yves Lendormy's renewed motion to correct/amend judgment is granted. Consistent with the Court's September 21, 2018 Order Confirming Arbitration Award, which awarded costs to Plaintiff, the Court's Judgment of the same date shall be amended to award Plaintiff costs in the amount of $21,856.62. (Code Civ. Proc. § 473(d); People v. Landon White Bail Bonds (1991) 234 Cal.App.3d 66, 69, 77 ["a judgment may be corrected at any time to reflect the trial court's manifest intent."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JEAN-YVES LENDORMY VS. KYLE HANSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax And Strike Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 2, 2019, line 3. DEFENDANTS KYLE HANSON, MARK MURPHY'S Motion To Tax And Strike Costs. Defendants Kyle Hanson and Mark Murphy's motion to strike or tax costs is denied. Plaintiff Jean-Yves Lendormy was the prevailing party in the action by virtue of this Court's September 21, 2018 Order Confirming Arbitration Award, which awarded Plaintiff costs, and the accompanying Judgment in his favor. (Code Civ. Proc. § 1032(a)(4) [defining "prevailing party" to include the party with a net monetary recovery]; Caro v. Smith (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 725, 736 ["'Under [Code of Civil Procedure] section 1287.4, judgment is entered when an award is confirmed; the costs provisions of section 1032 et seq. thus become applicable.'"].) Defendants' challenges to the specific items of costs claimed fail. Filing fees, deposition costs, and costs of service of process are all expressly authorized by statute. (Code Civ. Proc. § 1033.5(a)(1), (3)(A), & (4).) The Court finds that the costs of (i) models, enlargements, and photocopies of exhibits and (ii) Court Call appearances were reasonably necessary to the conduct of the underlying arbitration. (Code Civ. Proc. § 1033.5(c)(2).) Arbitrators' fees necessary for the conduct of civil litigation "constitute statutory costs that may be awarded under section 1033.5, subdivision (c)(4)." (Southern California Sunbelt Developers, Inc. v. Banyan Limited Partnership (2017) 8 Cal.App.5th 910, 931; see Cobler v. Stanley, Barber, Southard, Brown & Associates (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 518, 533 & n.9 [in contractual arbitration, prevailing party entitled to recover arbitration fees as costs].) Mr. Luscotoff's declaration establishes that Plaintiff paid a total of $21,288.22 to JAMS. (Luscotoff Decl. 4 & Ex. 3.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOHANNA WELTY VS. CRAIG W. MORRISON
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 2, 2019, line 4. DEFENDANT CRAIG MORRISON'S MOTION TO DISMISS. Defendant Craig W. Morrison's motion to dismiss the action pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 581(f)(2) is granted. Plaintiff failed to comply with the Court's November 29, 2018 Order, which granted her leave to file a second amended complaint by January 31, 2019, and has not filed any opposition to the motion. The Court is aware of Ms. Welty's "Fifth Declaration," filed with the Court on March 21, 2019. That declaration was presented for filing in the Court's case management department in response to a February 21, 2019 order to show cause, and was not filed in response to this motion. The physician's letter attached to the declaration, dated January 18, 2019, states only Ms. Welty "should be given some extra time to complete her work duties for this month," while she was being treated for a condition causing her visual impairment. Ms. Welty did not seek a further extension of time to file a second amended complaint, nor has she shown good cause for such an extension. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ANDRIY KRAVCHUK VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash The Defendants Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Directed To Airbnb, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 2, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF ANDRIY KRAVCHUK, ARTEM KOSHKALDA's Motion To Quash The Defendants Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Directed To Airbnb, Inc. Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to quash is DENIED. First, the notice of motion was not compliant with CCP section 1005 (insufficient notice). Second, movants did not meet and confer. Third, on the merits, the subpoena seeks records related to business transactions that are relevant to, or reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of information and potential witnesses relevant to, the claims and defenses of the parties. CCP § 2017.010. The Court does not find the privacy arguments well taken because the subpoena seeks records related to commercial transactions. AirBNB was served with the subpoena and did not object, so the Court declines to speculate as to any burden to be borne by AirBNB. Whether the evidence obtained will be admissible is not the question on whether it is discoverable. Williams v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 3 Cal.5th 531, 541. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN MOORE VS. LA-LA IMPORTS LP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And [Proposed] Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 2, 2019, line 6. PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And [Proposed] Consent Judgment. Plaintiff John Moore's motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement and proposed consent judgment is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2). rulings.Case Title: FABRICOR LLC ET AL VS. GUNEET BAJWA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 2, 2019, line 8. CROSS DEFENDANTS VIREO CAPITAL, INC., VENUSTUS HOTEL, LLC, SAMUEL LEIZOREK'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-defendants Vireo Capital, Inc., Venustus Hotel, LLC and Samuel Leizorke's demurrer to the cross-complaint is sustained without leave to amend based on misjoinder. (See Code Civ. Proc. § 430.10(d).) TPC St. Helena is the cross-complainant on the first and second causes of action and seeks to recover on its behalf as well as derivatively on behalf of Spring Mountain Hotel LLC. TPC has no right to maintain the cross-complaint because it is not "[a] party against whom a cause of action has been asserted in a complaint." (Code Civ. Proc. § 428.10.) TPC is an interloper. The third cause of action is similarly defective. In that claim, cross-complainants Patel, Bajwa, and TPC allege that Venustus Hotel, LLC breached the parties' buyout agreement. As explained, TPC has no right to maintain that claim. Patel and Bajwa's claim on the buyout agreement fails because it is not a claim against Fabricor LLC or Samuel Leizorek (Code Civ. Proc. § 428.10(a)) nor is it a proper claim under Code of Civil Procedure § 428.10(b) because it does not arise from the guaranty that is alleged in the complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for cross-defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
